title: centaurea aplolepa subsp. levantina
intro: Centaurea aplolepa subsp. levantina, also known as Levantine knapweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive leaf shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea aplolepa subsp. levantina are thought to include anti-anxiety and antioxidant effects, as well as the potential to lower cholesterol levels and protect against cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
